name: Ubaydullah ibn al-Abbas
title: Child of Ashura
shortDescription: One of the young sons of Hazrat Abbas (AS) who was present at Karbala and tasted the bitterness of his father's martyrdom.
fullStory:
Ubaydullah ibn al-Abbas was the son of Hazrat Abbas ibn Ali (AS) and Lubaba bint Ubaydullah ibn al-Abbas. He was a young child during the event of Ashura, present in the tents of the Ahl al-Bayt.

When Hazrat Abbas (AS) went towards the Euphrates to fetch water, Ubaydullah and the other thirsty children in the tents were waiting for their uncle and father's return. But upon hearing Imam Hussain's (AS) cry, 'Now my back is broken,' the hope in the tents turned to despair.

At a tender age, Ubaydullah witnessed the pain of becoming an orphan and the martyrdom of Karbala's bravest commander. After the tents were looted, he too was taken captive to Kufa and Sham with the caravan.

After returning to Medina, Ubaydullah grew up to become a learned and virtuous young man. He married the daughter of Imam Sajjad (AS), and the lineage of Hazrat Abbas (AS) continued exclusively through Ubaydullah. Many great scholars descended from his lineage.
quoteArabic: وَكانَ العَبّاسُ لَهُ ابْنٌ يُقالُ لَهُ عُبَيْدُاللَّهِ...
quote: (In genealogy books:) The lineage of Abbas ibn Ali continued exclusively through his son Ubaydullah.
